items.definition: Interoperability means different systems or components can work together by exchanging and actually using each other’s data or functionality, usually via agreed standards and protocols.
A value or choice that is not fixed or special in any way and may be any valid value of its type, so code must work correctly for all such possible values rather than relying on a particular one.
Immutability means that once a value or object is created, it cannot be changed; any “change” creates a new value instead of modifying the original.

In AI, reasoning simply means “thinking through a problem step by step to reach a sensible conclusion using the information it has”. It is how an AI goes beyond just memorizing patterns and actually works things out.
A frontier model is just one of the most powerful AI models in the world at the current time. It is “frontier” because it sits at the front edge of what AI can do right now.

MMLU(Massive Multitask Language Understanding) is basically a big exam for AI models that checks how good they are at many school and university subjects. It is used to compare how smart different language models are at general knowledge and problem‑solving.

Extended thinking is a mode where Claude spends more internal effort on a problem, generating a longer hidden chain of thought before responding. It’s like telling Claude, “Take your time and think step by step,” which often improves answers to complex questions.
Dynamically typed means Python determines a variable's type at runtime based on the value you assign, rather than requiring you to declare it explicitly.
Immutable means an object's state cannot be changed after creation. In Python, attempting to "modify" an immutable object actually creates a new object.
